title: How a man of God actually kills sin
caption:
Scripture doesn't say manage your sin. It says put it to death.

There's a difference between managing something and killing it. Managing it means you've made peace with it staying. Killing it means you've decided it doesn't get to.

Step one: name it without softening it. Not "I struggle with screens" — I am choosing pornography over Christ. The softened name keeps it comfortable. The real name makes it killable.

Step two: bring it into the light. "Confess your sins to one another, that you may be healed." — James 5:16. What you say out loud to a brother starts dying.

Step three: cut off the supply. You're not going to out-willpower your sin. Engineer the environment.

Step four: kill the desire, not just the action. Behavior is downstream of desire. Ask what the sin is giving you that you think you need — then find where God actually provides that.

What's one thing you've been managing that you need to kill?

#faith #scripture #christianmen #godlymen #manofgod #purity #discipline #christianity #james5
